title: Home - Ministry of Education
content:
- Collective agreements
Ngā kaupapa taumatua
- Attendance Going to school every day is important for students to develop skills for lifelong learning. Find out more
Going to school every day is important for students to develop skills for lifelong learning.
- Improving achievement We want all children to achieve their potential no matter where they are. Find out more

### Improving achievement
We want all children to achieve their potential no matter where they are.
- Learning support We're strengthening learning support so the education system meets the needs of all learners and their families. Find out more
We're strengthening learning support so the education system meets the needs of all learners and their families.
- Featured New timeframe for Years 0 to 8 final National Curriculum 14 May 2026 Schools and kura now have more time to start using the final Years 0 to 8 National Curriculum after a Government decision to further extend the implementation timeframe. Find out more

### New timeframe for Years 0 to 8 final National Curriculum
Schools and kura now have more time to start using the final Years 0 to 8 National Curriculum after a Government decision to further extend the implementation timeframe.
- Applications open for 2026-2027 Ngarimu VC and 28th (Māori) Battalion Memorial scholarships 8 June 2026 The 2026-2027 Ngarimu VC and 28th (Māori) Battalion Memorial scholarships and competitions are now open for applications. Find out more

### Applications open for 2026-2027 Ngarimu VC and 28th (Māori) Battalion Memorial scholarships
The 2026-2027 Ngarimu VC and 28th (Māori) Battalion Memorial scholarships and competitions are now open for applications.
- Budget 2026 28 May 2026 The Budget 2026 education package invests $2.1 billion over the next 4 years. Find out more
The Budget 2026 education package invests $2.1 billion over the next 4 years.
- Issue 5 of Education Gazette 2026 26 May 2026 In this fifth edition of 2026, we explore how teachers and school leaders in Aotearoa support students to grow as thinkers and problem-solvers. Find out more

### Issue 5 of Education Gazette 2026
In this fifth edition of 2026, we explore how teachers and school leaders in Aotearoa support students to grow as thinkers and problem-solvers.
Tukuna mai ō whakaaro
- First curriculum subjects – Years | Tau 11–13 open Closes: 15 June 2026 Consultation is now open on the first group of 26 subjects to be published in the new Years | Tau 11–13 curriculum, and we welcome feedback through to 15 June. Find out more

### First curriculum subjects – Years | Tau 11–13
Consultation is now open on the first group of 26 subjects to be published in the new Years | Tau 11–13 curriculum, and we welcome feedback through to 15 June.
- Consultation on second group of senior secondary curriculum subjects open Closes: 8 July 2026 The second group of subjects for Years 11 to 13 in the new curriculum is now available for you to review and provide your feedback. Find out more

### Consultation on second group of senior secondary curriculum subjects
The second group of subjects for Years 11 to 13 in the new curriculum is now available for you to review and provide your feedback.
- Motueka catchment enrolment scheme amendment open Closes: 14 June 2026 We are working with Brooklyn School, Lower Moutere School, Mahana School, Mapua School, Motueka South School, Riwaka School and Tasman School to remove gaps and overlaps between home zones. Find out more

### Motueka catchment enrolment scheme amendment
We are working with Brooklyn School, Lower Moutere School, Mahana School, Mapua School, Motueka South School, Riwaka School and Tasman School to remove gaps and overlaps between home zones.
